# epilot-environment_environment_variable (Resource)

EnvironmentVariable Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "epilot-environment_environment_variable" "my_environmentvariable" {
  description = "...my_description..."
  group       = "...my_group..."
  key         = "...my_key..."
  protected   = true
  type        = "String"
  value       = "...my_value..."
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) Environment variable key
- `type` (String) Type of variable. Used when creating a new variable. Defaults to String. must be one of ["String", "SecretString"]

### Optional

- `description` (String)
- `group` (String) Optional group name for organising variables in the UI
- `protected` (Boolean) Whether the variable is protected from editing
- `value` (String) Value is returned for String type, omitted for SecretString

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `updated_at` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = epilot-environment_environment_variable.my_epilot-environment_environment_variable
  id = "..."
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import epilot-environment_environment_variable.my_epilot-environment_environment_variable "..."
```
